_comment: Florida Educator Accomplished Practices (FEAPs). 6 practices with indicators per Rule 6A-5.065, F.A.C. Used by professional-learning and school-administration atoms for observation alignment. Source: FLDOE (revised June 2025). Public data.
source: Florida Department of Education. Rule 6A-5.065, F.A.C. https://www.fldoe.org/teaching/professional-dev/the-fl-educator-accomplished-practices.stml

practices.name: Instructional Design and Lesson Planning
practices.description: Applying concepts from human development and learning theories, the effective educator consistently designs standards-based lessons, plans instruction, and selects strategies to engage and meet the needs of learners.
indicators: Aligns instruction with state-adopted standards at the appropriate level of rigor
Sequences lessons and concepts to ensure coherence and required prior knowledge
Designs instruction for students to achieve mastery
Selects appropriate formative assessments to monitor learning
Uses diagnostic student data to plan lessons
Develops learning experiences that require students to demonstrate a variety of applicable skills and competencies

practices.name: The Learning Environment
practices.description: To maintain a student-centered learning environment that is safe, organized, equitable, flexible, inclusive, and collaborative, the effective educator consistently arranges and manages the physical and social conditions of the learning environment.
indicators: Organizes, allocates, and manages the resources of time, space, and attention
Manages individual and class behaviors through a well-planned management system
Conveys high expectations to all students
Respects students' cultural, linguistic, and family background
Models clear, acceptable oral and written communication skills
Maintains a climate of openness, inquiry, fairness, and support
Integrates current information and communication technologies
Adapts the learning environment to accommodate the differing needs and diversity of students
Utilizes current and emerging assistive technologies that enable students to participate in high-quality communication and interaction

practices.name: Instructional Delivery and Facilitation
practices.description: The effective educator consistently utilizes a deep and comprehensive knowledge of the subject taught to deliver engaging and relevant lessons and facilitate student achievement.
indicators: Delivers engaging and challenging lessons
Deepens and enriches students' understanding through content area literacy strategies, verbalization of thought, and application of the subject matter
Identifies gaps in students' subject matter knowledge
Modifies instruction to respond to preconceptions or misconceptions
Relates and integrates the subject matter with other disciplines and life experiences
Employs higher-order questioning techniques
Applies varied instructional strategies and resources, including appropriate technology
Differentiates instruction based on an assessment of student learning needs and recognition of individual differences
Supports, encourages, and provides immediate and specific feedback to students to promote student achievement
Utilizes student feedback to monitor instructional needs and to adjust instruction

practices.name: Assessment
practices.description: The effective educator consistently gathers, analyzes, and uses data and evidence to inform decisions about teaching and learning.
indicators: Analyzes and applies data from multiple assessments and measures
Designs and aligns formative and summative assessments that match learning objectives and lead to mastery
Uses a variety of assessment tools to monitor student progress, achievement, and learning gains
Modifies assessments and testing conditions to accommodate learning styles and varying levels of knowledge
Shares the importance and outcomes of student assessment data with the student and the student's parent/caregiver(s)
Applies technology to organize and integrate assessment information

practices.name: Continuous Professional Improvement
practices.description: The effective educator consistently designs purposeful professional goals to strengthen the effectiveness of instruction based on students' needs.
indicators: Designs purposeful professional goals to strengthen the effectiveness of instruction based on students' needs
Examines and uses data-informed research to improve instruction and student achievement
Uses a variety of data, independently and in collaboration with colleagues, to evaluate learning outcomes, adjust planning, and continuously improve the effectiveness of lessons
Collaborates with the home, school, and larger communities to foster communication and to support student learning and continuous improvement
Applies knowledge of emerging trends in education to enhance professional practice

practices.name: Professional Responsibility and Ethical Conduct
practices.description: Understanding that educators are held to a high moral standard in a community, the effective educator adheres to the Code of Ethics and the Principles of Professional Conduct of the Education Profession of Florida.
indicators: Understands and adheres to the Code of Ethics and Principles of Professional Conduct
Fulfills the expected obligations to students, the public, and the education profession
